trigger: LLM generated score validation assertions using exact equality on floating point computed values
symptom: Tests fail non-deterministically across Python versions, platforms, or when model changes produce slightly different scores; assert 0.75 == 0.7500000000001
bad_code:
# tests/test_scoring.py (pattern ~20 times across test suite)
assert result['novelty_score'] == 0.75
assert result['feasibility_score'] == 0.5
assert combined == novelty * feasibility * impact   # exact float multiply
good_code:
# tests/test_scoring.py
import math
assert math.isclose(result['novelty_score'], 0.75, rel_tol=1e-6)
assert 0.0 <= result['feasibility_score'] <= 1.0   # range check often better
assert math.isclose(combined, novelty * feasibility * impact, rel_tol=1e-9)
notes: Exact float equality fails on rounding differences between CPU/GPU, different numpy versions, or model output precision. Use math.isclose(), pytest.approx(), or range assertions for computed scores.
